steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes “the similarity of the ______ experiences.” The passage refers to two novels (Northanger Abbey and The Mysteries of Udolpho) and two protagonists (Catherine Morland and Emily St. Aubert). Both nouns before “experiences” must be plural and possessive.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — novels' protagonists: “novels'” is correct (plural possessive), but “protagonists” without an apostrophe is not possessive — it doesn't show that the experiences belong to the protagonists. ✗
  • Choice B — novels' protagonists': Both plural possessives: the protagonists belong to the novels, the experiences belong to the protagonists. Matches two novels and two protagonists. ✓
  • Choice C — novel's protagonist's: Both singular, but the passage explicitly names two novels and two protagonists. ✗
  • Choice D — novel's protagonists': Singular “novel's” contradicts the two novels referenced in the passage. ✗
B is correct.
